title: Sequence-specific features of short double-strand, blunt-end RNAs have RIG-I and type 1 interferon-dependent or -independent antiviral effects
author: Suomalainen, Maarit
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Pathogen-associated molecular patterns trigger induction of interferon (IFN) via pattern recognition receptors such as RIG-I and induce anti-viral states into infected and surrounding non-infected cells. Here we describe that that transfection of human airway cell lines and primary fibroblasts with a short, blunt-end dsRNA mimicking the cellular miRNA miR29b-1* gives strong anti-viral effects against a DNA virus AdV-C5 and against RNA viruses IAV and Sars-CoV-2, whereas as a mimic with a scrambled miR29b-1* sequence failed to induce anti-viral state to the mimic-transfected cell. These anti-viral effects of miR29b-1* sequence required blunt-end complementary RNA strands and were mediated through RIG-I and IFNAR2.
steps_raw: Pathogen-associated molecular patterns trigger induction of interferon (IFN) via pattern recognition receptors such as RIG-I and induce anti-viral states into infected and surrounding non-infected cells. These anti-viral effects of miR29b-1* sequence required blunt-end complementary RNA strands and were mediated through RIG-I and IFNAR2.
